text: Lost this exact line to Roman Wednesday. Fix the move order.
Rook behind the pawn. Drilled it. Wont happen again.
5...Na5, not Nxd5. Burned in now.
Still hate the c8 bishop. Need a real plan for it.
Only first six moves. Stop overpreparing.
Opposition off the board, not the screen. Slow down.
Stop playing 6.Bg5 from memory. Know why the pin matters first.
English Attack: g4 push too early last game. Castle long first.
Rubinstein is fine for the club. Trade the bad bishop, sit on the c-file.
Caro-Kann Advance: light-square bishop has to get out before c3. Note this.
Berlin endgame is dry but holds. Good against Roman's pressure.
KIA against the French setup. Reti's plan, e4 break when ready.
QGD as White: minority attack on the queenside. Practiced it twice.
London is my tired-night opening. Bf4, e3, c3, done. No thinking.
Yugoslav Attack: never trade the dark-square bishop on h6 for free. Lost a game to that.
Five-zero blitz sweep. Same Italian setup every game. Tactics held up.
White repertoire for the Open: 1.d4, London and QGD only. Keep it narrow.
Lucena and Philidor cold. Drilled both off the board until I stopped checking.
Opposite-color bishops, down a pawn, still a draw if I hold the right diagonal.
Nimzowitsch on prophylaxis. Ask what the opponent wants, then stop it. Reread chapter.
Simul against the club juniors. Keep positions simple, one plan per board.
Alapin 2.c3 if I don't want theory. Closed Sicilian if I want a slow game.
